question: The word 'track' as a verb means to ________.
choices: monitor or follow the progress or movement of something
store data in a cloud server
embed a sensor into the body

question: In the passive voice, when we use it we are focusing more on ________.
Read: 'It should not be used as protection from wild animals.' This uses which passive form?
Read: 'A transmitter is worn on the body and digital messages are sent via the body's electrical field.' Why is the agent not mentioned?
choices: It is unknown who wears it
It is obvious that a person wears it
It is illegal to mention the agent

question: In the speaking box for describing trends, the phrase 'In this graph, we can see …' is used to ________.
In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', early adopters tend to be ________.
The word 'upgrade' means ________.
choices: to change for something newer or better

question: According to the Active Vocabulary section, 'gadgets' and 'devices' tend to be ________ than appliances.

Read from the VR article: 'I bump into a friend, but I can't stop to talk to her because it's all so overwhelming. I need to calm down.' This opening paragraph describes a person who is experiencing ________.
choices: excitement in a busy shopping centre

question: Read from the VR article: 'While people's experiences vary a lot, one of the key issues for many individuals with autism is a hypersensitivity to sights, sounds, smells and tastes, which can make a simple activity, such as going shopping, extremely ________.'
Read from the VR article: 'This can make it difficult to fit in with or to keep friends because other people just don't understand the pressure.' 'Keep friends' here means ________.
choices: to maintain existing friendships over time
to find friends with similar interests

question: Read: 'In order to help people understand what it's like to have autism, could Virtual Reality (VR) help? Virtual Reality is a computer-generated simulation of an experience, which is seen in 3D.' What does 'simulation' mean here?
choices: A live broadcast of a real event
An imitation or artificial recreation of an experience
A digital game or entertainment system

question: Read from the VR article: 'But for others, they end up having a meltdown — in other words, they get upset or angry.' What does 'meltdown' mean here?
choices: A technical failure of a device
An emotional breakdown or loss of control
A physical collapse due to exhaustion

question: The phrasal verb 'come up against' means ________.
choices: to face or encounter a difficulty or obstacle

question: The phrasal verb 'break off' means ________.
choices: to stop or interrupt an activity

question: Read from the VR article: 'Some people went through the virtual experience of chopping down a tree. Afterwards, the researcher accidentally spilt his drink and those people reached for fewer paper napkins.' What is the conclusion of this experiment?
choices: People became more careless after the VR experience
VR experiences can subconsciously encourage people to change their real-world behaviour
People enjoyed the VR tree-chopping experience
VR makes people more aware of environmental issues consciously

question: Read: 'We volunteer to put on the VR headset. We can break off at any time and go back to our normal lives, whereas the people whose lives we're experiencing don't have that luxury.' What does this comparison show?
choices: VR is more realistic than real life
There is a fundamental difference between simulated and actual experiences
VR headsets are uncomfortable to wear for long periods
People who use VR feel more empathy automatically

question: Read from the VR article comprehension questions: 'Why does the writer start by describing an experience of going shopping?' The correct answer is ________.
choices: to explain exactly why some people suffer from ASD
to demonstrate why having ASD can cause someone to get upset
to emphasise the social problems created by large shopping malls
to introduce the concept of virtual reality

question: Read from the VR article comprehension questions: 'What attitude towards VR is expressed by the writer in the last paragraph?' The correct answer is ________.
choices: It is a passing fashion that will disappear
It has some value but should be treated with caution
It is better than real-life experience

question: The word 'superficial' means ________.
choices: related only to the surface or appearance

question: Read from the VR article: 'It can also lead to people with autism having problems at school or with holding down a job, as they may come across as unreliable or bad-tempered.' 'Come across as' means ________.
choices: to appear to others in a certain way

question: Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'In spite of these potential benefits, a significant number of people are choosing to use ad-blocking software.' What does this sentence introduce?
choices: An argument in favour of data tracking
An argument against data tracking despite the benefits mentioned

question: Which linker is classified as a 'conjunction of concession'?
Which linker is classified as an 'adverb of contrast'?
According to the Life Skills section on social media, social media sites can track the user's ________.
According to the Life Skills section on managing your online image, you should do a search on your name every ________ months or so.

Read: 'Once something is posted, it is never completely gone, because any information posted on the Internet can be archived or saved in a number of different ways.' According to this passage, once something is posted online it ________.
choices: is automatically removed after six months

question: Read: 'Basically, you should behave online as you would in real life.' This means that ________.
choices: online rules are stricter than real-life rules
the same rules that apply in real life apply online

question: Choose the correct passive form: 'The fridge ________ to reorder food as you use it.'
Read: 'A good rule of thumb is to ask yourself how you would feel if your grandmother saw what you've shared or posted!' The purpose of this advice is to ________.
choices: make people feel guilty about their posts
encourage people to share everything with family
help people think carefully before posting anything online
remind people to set up privacy controls

question: In the impersonal passive grammar, Pattern (c) uses: subject + passive (be + past participle) + ________.
choices: perfect infinitive (to have been + past participle)

question: Rewrite using Pattern (b): 'People say that a scientist called Elisha Gray invented the phone at the same time as Bell.' → A scientist called Elisha Gray ________ to have invented the phone at the same time as Bell.
Read from the Bell article: 'It has often been remarked that Alexander did not like to have a telephone in his study because it disturbed him from his work!' Why is this surprising?
choices: Because he worked very long hours
Because he was the inventor of the telephone himself
Because he had many telephones at home
Because he needed to make business calls

question: Read from the Bell article: 'Antonio Meucci was widely reported to have invented a telephone in 1856, 20 years before Alexander.' This means Meucci's invention was in ________.
In the essay writing box, which phrase is used to introduce the topic in the essay introduction?
choices: In conclusion, it seems obvious that …
In spite of these potential benefits …
These days it is extremely common for companies to …

question: Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'Most people really have very little idea how much data is being given away every time they click, or what that data will be used for.' This is an argument ________ data tracking.
choices: against, because people lack awareness and control
supporting, because data helps companies improve services

question: Choose the correct form: 'Studies ________ out which claim that it will revolutionise our lives.'
In the passive grammar, 'A transmitter is worn on the body' uses the ________ passive.
According to the Life Skills section on social media, one of the negative effects of spending too much time on social media is that users think ________.
choices: other people are having a better or more exciting time than they are

question: Read from the Bell article: 'Today, it is estimated that there are more phones in the world than people!' This means the number of phones ________ the number of people.
